mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output that boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.5% indicates sufficient labor market slack to trigger downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 5.5% during any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month rise in the federal funds rate and a month-over-month fall in retail sales indicate monetary over-tightening into a consumption slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month and RSAFS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, a month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects construction-led demand acceleration that lifts broad corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns follow fewer than 60% of month-over-month HOUST increases in identified post-recession recovery quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates rising labor income that feeds immediately into equity fund inflows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS increases precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 52% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 5.0% applies a restrictive discount rate to equity cash flows that overwhelms near-term earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of trading days when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production confirm a broad contraction across demand and supply, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month and INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to precede negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a sustained manufacturing contraction that triggers rapid de-risking, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns follow fewer than 55% of instances after two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines over a 2-year rolling window.
